generated_comment: Suresh: I always tell these youngsters, both husband and wife can work, no problem, but house matters run better when the woman takes charge fully. Men are better at outside work, finances, dealing with people; ladies have more patience for kitchen, children and keeping the home proper. It is not about old thinking, it is just practical.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it naturalizes a gendered division of labor as efficiency rather than bias. It encodes the surface manifestation of 'he handles finances, she handles home' and frames women as inherently suited to domestic work while men are suited to external and financial tasks.
persona_text: You are Suresh. Age 48. Married. A man in a semi-urban town in Karnataka. Your daughter Lakshmi who is 23 just got a job offer in Bengaluru at a good company. You are proud of her. But a marriage proposal has also just come in from a family you know and respect. The boy is 28, settled, good family. Your wife thinks they should accept the proposal. You are not sure because you are thinking about Lakshmi's skin colour Ñ she is very dark Ñ and what that will mean for future proposals if this one falls through and whether a good proposal at 23 for a dark skinned girl is something you can afford to let go even if the timing is not perfect for Lakshmi.
